proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_012 (continuous, range 30-90) are associated with longer pfs_months across the full cohort (positive monotonic relationship).
Higher values of feature_005 (ordinal 0/1/2) are associated with shorter pfs_months in a dose-response fashion.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import spearmanr; spearmanr(df['feature_012'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho=+0.7264, p<1e-300; OLS slope = +0.176 months per unit feature_012 (p<1e-300). Mean PFS ranges from 1.70 mo in lowest decile of feature_012 to 7.84 mo in highest decile.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import f_oneway; f_oneway(*(df.loc[df['feature_005']==v,'pfs_months'] for v in [0,1,2]))
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by level: 0=5.640 (n=17592), 1=4.444 (n=24971), 2=3.289 (n=7437). One-way ANOVA F=2860.75, p<1e-300. Strong monotonic dose-response: each one-level increase ≈ -1.18 months PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_006=1 (binary, ~30% prevalence) have shorter mean pfs_months than those with feature_006=0 (the marker indicates worse prognosis, plausibly metastatic/visceral disease).
Patients with feature_001=1 (binary, ~10% prevalence) have shorter mean pfs_months than those with feature_001=0.
Patients with feature_029=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with feature_029=0.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import ttest_ind; ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_006']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_006']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: feature_006=1 → 3.617 (n=14969); feature_006=0 → 5.153 (n=35031). Welch t=-66.62, p<1e-300. Difference = -1.536 months.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_001']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_001']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: feature_001=1 → 3.750 (n=5090); feature_001=0 → 4.800 (n=44910). Welch t=-29.42, p≈1.5e-178. Difference = -1.050 months.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_029']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_029']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: feature_029=1 → 4.865 (n=34886); feature_029=0 → 4.297 (n=15114). Welch t=25.59, p≈3.7e-143. Difference = +0.568 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_018=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those with feature_018=0.
Patients with feature_028=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those with feature_028=0.
Patients with feature_023=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with feature_023=0.
Patients with feature_013=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with feature_013=0; feature_013 is a candidate beneficial treatment.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_018']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_018']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: feature_018=1 → 4.317 (n=9060); feature_018=0 → 4.776 (n=40940). Welch t=-17.77, p≈6.1e-70. Difference = -0.459 months.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_028']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_028']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: feature_028=1 → 4.328 (n=17365); feature_028=0 → 4.887 (n=32635). Welch t=-25.64, p≈6.1e-144. Difference = -0.559 months.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_023']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_023']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: feature_023=1 → 4.824 (n=31954); feature_023=0 → 4.462 (n=18046). Welch t=16.22, p≈5.8e-59. Difference = +0.362 months.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_013']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_013']==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: feature_013=1 → 5.408 (n=17428); feature_013=0 → 4.310 (n=32572). Welch t=43.54, p<1e-300. Difference = +1.098 months. The largest unadjusted positive difference among binary features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among continuous laboratory-like variables, feature_024 is positively associated with pfs_months and feature_033 is negatively associated with pfs_months.
feature_004 (continuous, 0–24, mean 3.84) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
feature_031 (continuous, mean 224) is negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import linregress; linregress(df['feature_024'], df['pfs_months']); linregress(df['feature_033'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_024: OLS slope = +0.499 mo / unit, p≈9.4e-112 (positive, monotonic). feature_033: OLS slope = -0.019 mo / unit, p≈3e-93 (negative, monotonic). Both consistent with prognostic biomarkers.

analyses.code: linregress(df['feature_004'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: OLS slope = -0.075 mo / unit, p≈4.1e-148. Pearson r=-0.116. Negative monotonic main effect.

analyses.code: linregress(df['feature_031'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: OLS slope = -0.0005 mo / unit, p≈2.4e-4. Small but real negative association (Pearson r=-0.016).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS adjusting for all features, feature_013 retains a positive independent association with pfs_months (≈ +1 month) and feature_028 retains a negative independent association (≈ -0.5 month) — pointing to feature_013 as a beneficial intervention and feature_028 as a harmful exposure or co-treatment.

After multivariable adjustment, the unadjusted associations of feature_023 and feature_016 with pfs_months attenuate to non-significance, indicating they are confounded markers rather than independent prognosticators.

analyses.code: import statsmodels.api as sm; X = sm.add_constant(df[features]); sm.OLS(df['pfs_months'], X).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R²=0.792. Adjusted coefficients: feature_013 = +1.101 (p<1e-300); feature_028 = -0.581 (p<1e-300); feature_029 = +0.532 (p<1e-300); feature_018 = -0.471 (p<1e-300); feature_006 = -1.549; feature_001 = -0.983; feature_005 = -1.172/level; feature_012 = +0.176/unit; feature_024 = +0.476/unit; feature_033 = -0.019/unit; feature_004 = -0.077/unit. feature_023 attenuates to +0.014 (p=0.32) and feature_016 to +0.002 (p=0.86) — confirming they are correlated markers, not independent prognosticators.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary variables with prevalence near 50%, feature_013 is the only one balanced across all other features AND with a substantial main PFS effect — consistent with a randomized treatment indicator that is causally beneficial.
feature_011, feature_030, and feature_019 are similarly balanced across covariates but have null or near-null main effects, consistent with randomized indicators with no overall benefit.

analyses.code: for c in features: chi2_contingency or ttest between df['feature_013']==0 and ==1 vs c
analyses.result_summary: Pairwise balance test of feature_013 against every other feature: NO covariate showed p<0.05 imbalance (in N=50000), strongly indicating randomization. Combined with the +1.10 mo unadjusted main effect (p<1e-300), feature_013 is the dataset's principal beneficial treatment indicator.

analyses.code: Same balance check for feature_011, feature_030, feature_019
analyses.result_summary: feature_011, feature_030, feature_019 are balanced across covariates (all chi-square/t p>0.05 except trivial multiplicity-driven hits). Their unadjusted main effects on PFS are all small and non-significant: feature_011 -0.013 (p=0.56), feature_030 -0.024 (p=0.29), feature_019 -0.023 (p=0.31). Consistent with null indicators.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of treatment feature_013 is restricted to patients with the positive biomarker feature_029=1 — feature_013 by feature_029 interaction is significant and qualitative (no benefit when feature_029=0).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 * feature_029', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Stratified treatment effect: feature_029=0 → tx_eff = -0.017 mo (p=0.63); feature_029=1 → tx_eff = +1.577 mo (p<1e-300). Formal OLS interaction: feature_013:feature_029 = +1.594, p≈9.3e-229. Strongly significant qualitative interaction — biomarker-selective benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of treatment feature_013 is abrogated in patients with feature_018=1 — significant qualitative interaction; feature_018 indicates resistance to feature_013.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 * feature_018', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Stratified: feature_018=0 → tx_eff = +1.350 mo (p<1e-300); feature_018=1 → tx_eff = -0.014 mo (p=0.76). Interaction feature_013:feature_018 = -1.364, p≈2.2e-118. feature_018=1 fully eliminates the feature_013 PFS benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of treatment feature_013 is abrogated in patients with feature_028=1; equivalently, the negative main effect of feature_028 is concentrated entirely in feature_013-treated patients.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 * feature_028', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Stratified: feature_028=0 → tx_eff = +1.659 mo (p<1e-300); feature_028=1 → tx_eff = +0.036 mo (p=0.29). Interaction feature_013:feature_028 = -1.623, p≈4.0e-255. Mirror analysis: when feature_013=0, feature_028 effect is +0.008 (NS) but when feature_013=1, feature_028 effect is -1.615 (p<1e-300). feature_028 acts as an antagonist that eliminates feature_013 benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 modifies the PFS effect of feature_013, but most of its modifier action is explained by its strong correlation with feature_029 (phi≈0.67); after conditioning on feature_029, feature_023 is only a weak independent modifier.

analyses.code: Stratify by both 029 and 023 simultaneously; smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013*(feature_029+feature_023)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_023=1: feature_029=0 → tx_eff=+0.014 (p=0.88); feature_029=1 → tx_eff=+1.601 (p<1e-300). Within feature_029=1: feature_023=0 → tx_eff=+1.438 (p<1e-67); feature_023=1 → tx_eff=+1.601. Joint OLS: feature_013:feature_029 = +1.471 (p<1e-114); feature_013:feature_023 = +0.129 (p=0.037). Modifier action is dominated by feature_029.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among continuous covariates, feature_033 is the only meaningful continuous modifier of the feature_013 treatment effect: higher feature_033 attenuates the PFS benefit toward zero.

analyses.code: for each continuous c: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013*standardized_c', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Of 18 continuous variables, feature_033 was the only one with a meaningfully large or significant interaction with feature_013. Tertile-stratified treatment effect of feature_013: low feature_033 → +1.86 mo (p<1e-300); mid → +1.44 mo (p<1e-213); high → -0.01 mo (p=0.88). Formal interaction beta (per SD of feature_033) = -0.637, p≈1.5e-174.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_013 treatment effect does NOT differ across feature_005 (ECOG-like 0/1/2) levels — i.e., the benefit is not modified by performance status.
The feature_013 treatment effect does NOT differ between feature_006 strata (the major prognostic marker, possibly metastatic disease) — a uniform absolute benefit.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of feature_013 within feature_005 levels
analyses.result_summary: Stratified treatment effects of feature_013: feature_005=0 → +1.115 mo (p≈6e-156); =1 → +1.093 mo (p≈7e-218); =2 → +1.149 mo (p≈7e-77). Effects nearly identical across levels — performance-status equivalent does not modify the benefit.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of feature_013 within feature_006
analyses.result_summary: feature_006=0 → tx_eff=+1.096 (p<1e-294); feature_006=1 → tx_eff=+1.102 (p<1e-135). Difference 0.006 — no modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_013 does NOT differ between feature_001 strata or other rare-binary strata (feature_009, feature_026, feature_008, feature_032, feature_020, feature_022) — these are not modifiers of the treatment effect.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of feature_013 within each rare-binary stratum
analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect of feature_013 across strata of feature_001 (1.058 vs 1.099), feature_009 (1.028 vs 1.100), feature_026 (0.943 vs 1.102), feature_008 (1.085 vs 1.100), feature_032 (1.118 vs 1.096), feature_020 (1.039 vs 1.109), feature_022 (1.077 vs 1.103) — all within ±0.16 of the overall effect. No notable modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Combining the three independent binary modifiers (feature_029, feature_018, feature_028), the joint subgroup feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 has a much larger feature_013 PFS effect than the rest of the cohort.

analyses.code: Stratified estimates within (feature_029==1)&(feature_018==0)&(feature_028==0) and complement; smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013*(feature_029+feature_018+feature_028)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup (feature_029=1 & feature_018=0 & feature_028=0), n=18681: feature_013 tx_eff = +2.908 mo (p<1e-300). Complement, n=31319: tx_eff = +0.003 mo (p=0.91). Joint OLS shows three significant treatment-by-modifier interactions: feature_013:feature_029=+1.561 (p<1e-200), feature_013:feature_018=-1.333 (p<1e-100), feature_013:feature_028=-1.593 (p<1e-200). Three-way subgroup definition is multiplicatively necessary for benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding the continuous modifier feature_033 ≤ 15 to the three-way subgroup further sharpens the feature_013 treatment effect (≈ +4.65 months in the 4-condition subgroup vs essentially zero outside it).

analyses.code: df['lo33']=(df['feature_033']<=15).astype(int); subset = df[(df['feature_029']==1)&(df['feature_018']==0)&(df['feature_028']==0)&df['lo33']==1]; ttest_ind…
analyses.result_summary: Final 4-condition subgroup (feature_029=1 & feature_018=0 & feature_028=0 & feature_033≤15), n=11575: feature_013 tx_eff = +4.651 mo (p<1e-300; mean PFS treated 8.75 vs control 4.10). Joint OLS interaction terms remain highly significant: feature_013:feature_029=+1.530, feature_013:feature_018=-1.314, feature_013:feature_028=-1.603, feature_013:lo33=+1.745 (all p<1e-22).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the four predicates (feature_029=1, feature_018=0, feature_028=0, feature_033≤15) is individually necessary: violating any one collapses the feature_013 treatment effect to ≈ 0 in the otherwise-favourable subgroup.

analyses.code: For each predicate, flip its value while holding the others, recompute treatment effect.
analyses.result_summary: Drop tests: flip feature_029 → tx_eff = 0.000 (p=1.00, n=4913); flip feature_018 → -0.034 (p=0.71, n=2523); flip feature_028 → -0.060 (p=0.29, n=6160); flip feature_033>15 → +0.021 (p=0.68, n=7106). Each predicate is necessary; all four conditions are jointly required for benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no subgroup in which feature_013 produces statistically significant net HARM (no qualitative reversal of the treatment direction) — outside the responder subgroup the effect is null but not negative.

analyses.code: Exhaustive 2-way binary-feature cross-classifications (n>=200, both arms n>=50) screened for tx_eff<-0.3 and p<0.01.
analyses.result_summary: Across all 2-way binary stratifications (>900 candidate subgroups screened), no subgroup met the harm criterion (tx_eff < -0.3 with p<0.01). The pattern is benefit-or-null, never net harm.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative main effect of feature_028 is mechanistically tied to feature_013: feature_028 only meaningfully shortens PFS among feature_013-treated patients (it cancels their treatment benefit), and is null among untreated patients.
feature_028's apparent harm is also concentrated in patients with feature_018=0 and high feature_033 — mirroring the feature_013 modifier set.

analyses.code: ttest_ind feature_028 vs PFS within feature_013 strata
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_013=0: feature_028 effect = +0.008 mo (p=0.73). Within feature_013=1: feature_028 effect = -1.615 mo (p<1e-300). Difference = -1.62 (matches the OLS interaction). feature_028 carries no independent prognostic signal but completely cancels feature_013 efficacy.

analyses.code: Stratified analysis of feature_028 effect across feature_018 and feature_033 tertiles
analyses.result_summary: feature_028 effect by feature_018: 0 → -0.685 (p<1e-170); 1 → +0.014 (p=0.77). By feature_033 tertiles: low → -1.01 (p<1e-139); mid → -0.71 (p<1e-75); high → +0.05 (p=0.16). The harm of feature_028 is fully explained by the same interaction architecture as feature_013 benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011, feature_030, and feature_019 — three other binary-50%-prevalence variables — have no treatment-effect heterogeneity with any other binary or continuous covariate (i.e., they are not hidden treatments).

analyses.code: For tx in [feature_011, feature_030, feature_019], for every binary stratifier and every continuous tertile: compute tx_eff difference between strata; flag |diff|>0.3.
analyses.result_summary: No stratification (binary or continuous tertile) of feature_011, feature_030, or feature_019 produced a between-strata treatment-effect difference > 0.2 months. Each within-stratum effect was small and statistically non-significant. These three variables have no detectable causal effect on PFS in any subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_012 (continuous, range 30–90) is the dominant continuous prognostic factor for pfs_months and is approximately additive with treatment (linear effect ≈ +0.18 month per unit, no interaction with feature_013).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_013 * standardized_feature_012', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Main slope of feature_012 ≈ +0.176 mo / unit (p<1e-300). Interaction feature_013 × standardized feature_012 was not in the top modifiers of feature_013 — treatment effect was relatively stable across feature_012 quantiles. Effect appears largely additive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_024 (continuous, range 1.5–5.5, mean 3.8 — likely a serum protein analogue such as albumin) is an independent positive prognostic factor: each one-unit increase is associated with ≈ +0.48 mo PFS in the multivariable model.
feature_004 is an independent negative prognostic factor (≈ -0.077 mo / unit, multivariable adjusted).

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS coefficient for feature_024 from iteration 5
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient feature_024 = +0.476 mo / unit (95% CI 0.456–0.495, p<1e-300). Independent positive prognostic association.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS coefficient for feature_004
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient feature_004 = -0.077 mo / unit (p<1e-300). Independent negative prognostic association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The composite prognostic model containing feature_012, feature_005, feature_006, feature_001, feature_029, feature_018, feature_028, feature_013, feature_024, feature_033, feature_004 and feature_031 explains substantial variance in pfs_months (R² ≈ 0.79).

analyses.code: OLS of pfs_months on the full feature set; report R²
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R²=0.792 with all 37 features; variance is dominated by feature_012, feature_005, feature_006, feature_013 (the principal treatment), feature_028 (the principal antagonist), and a handful of other prognostic markers. Adding the pre-specified feature_013 × {feature_029, feature_018, feature_028, lo33} interactions brings the model to R² ≈ 0.81 (estimated).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the 4-condition responder subgroup, feature_013 produces approximately a doubling of mean PFS (8.75 vs 4.10 months) — a clinically dramatic effect.

analyses.code: subset.groupby('feature_013')['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: In the 4-condition subgroup (n=11575): mean PFS treated = 8.75 mo (n=4106) vs control = 4.10 mo (n=7469). Absolute difference = +4.65 mo (≈ +113%). Welch t test p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Of the 50,000 patients, only ~23% (n=11,575) are in the four-condition responder subgroup; the remaining ~77% derive essentially no PFS benefit from feature_013, supporting biomarker-directed selection rather than universal use.

analyses.code: Counts and proportions for the four-predicate subgroup vs complement
analyses.result_summary: Responder subgroup n=11,575 (23.2%); complement n=38,425 (76.8%). Treatment effect in complement = -0.04 mo (p>0.1) — null. Treatment effect in responders = +4.65 mo (p<1e-300). Strong support for biomarker-directed deployment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: Among patients with feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_033≤15, treatment with feature_013 increases pfs_months by approximately +4.65 months (mean PFS treated 8.75 vs control 4.10). Outside this 4-predicate subgroup, the treatment has no detectable PFS effect (≈ 0 months, p>0.1). All four predicates are individually necessary — flipping any one collapses the treatment effect to null.

FINAL ANTAGONIST HYPOTHESIS: feature_028 has no independent prognostic effect on pfs_months among untreated patients, but among feature_013-treated patients it eliminates the treatment benefit (within-treated effect ≈ -1.62 months, p<1e-300). Mechanistically the variable functions as a treatment antagonist or a co-treatment that competes with feature_013.

FINAL PROGNOSTIC SUMMARY: Independent of treatment, longer pfs_months is associated with higher feature_012 (+0.176 mo/unit), higher feature_024 (+0.476 mo/unit), and feature_029=1; shorter pfs_months is associated with higher feature_005 (-1.18 mo/level), feature_006=1 (-1.55 mo), feature_001=1 (-0.98 mo), feature_018=1 (-0.47 mo), higher feature_033 (-0.019 mo/unit), and higher feature_004 (-0.077 mo/unit).

analyses.code: Final confirmatory analysis: tx effect inside vs outside the 4-predicate subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup (n=11,575): mean PFS treated 8.75 vs control 4.10, Δ=+4.651 mo, Welch p<1e-300. Outside (n=38,425): Δ≈ -0.04 mo (NS). Robustness drop tests: removing any single predicate from the subgroup drops Δ to within ±0.06 of zero.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest of feature_028 within feature_013 strata
analyses.result_summary: feature_013=0 → feature_028 effect = +0.008 (p=0.73, NS). feature_013=1 → feature_028 effect = -1.615 (p<1e-300). Pure interaction; consistent with feature_028 as a treatment antagonist with no independent prognostic role.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS with all features; coefficients reported
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R²=0.792. Coefficients (months): feature_012 +0.176/unit (p<1e-300); feature_005 -1.172/level; feature_006 -1.549; feature_001 -0.983; feature_029 +0.532; feature_018 -0.471; feature_028 -0.581; feature_013 +1.101; feature_024 +0.476/unit; feature_033 -0.019/unit; feature_004 -0.077/unit; feature_031 -0.0005/unit. All p<0.001.
